sections.paragraph: (for מְאוּם, מנוּם, from the root מָאַם), m. spot, a stain, blemish (Syr. ܡܘܽܡܳܐ id., Arab. مِيمُ and موم a spot; specially of small pox; Gr. μῶμος.-(a) in a physical sense used of any defect or blemish of body, Lev 21:17, sq.; 22:20 , 21 Lev 22:21, 25 Lev 22:25. Those who are spoken of as beautiful, should be free from every blemish, 2Sa 14:25 Son 4:7.-(b) in a moral sense, Deu 32:5 Job 11:15 31:7 .
